questions.question: What should an Angular route guard return to redirect the user to /login instead of just blocking navigation?
options: false
null
router.createUrlTree(['/login'])
new RedirectCommand('/login')

questions.question: In a functional CanActivateFn guard, how do you access services like AuthService?
options: Pass them as constructor parameters to the guard function
Use inject() inside the guard function body — functional guards run in an injection context
Import a global singleton from the service file directly
Use the route.data property to retrieve injected services

questions.question: A route has canActivate: [authGuard, adminGuard]. What happens if authGuard returns false?
options: adminGuard still runs to give it a chance to override
Both guards run in parallel and results are combined
Angular short-circuits: adminGuard does not run, navigation is blocked
Angular throws an error because guards must not conflict

questions.question: Which guard type would you use to warn a user 'You have unsaved changes' when they try to navigate away from a form?
options: canActivate
canMatch
canDeactivate
resolve

questions.question: What is the advantage of using a resolve guard over loading data inside ngOnInit?
options: resolve guards are faster because they bypass the HTTP cache
resolve guards prefetch data before the component renders, eliminating loading flickers in the template
resolve guards automatically retry failed requests; ngOnInit does not
resolve guards work only with lazy-loaded routes; ngOnInit works everywhere
